tasks.title: Write Project Control Usage Guide
tasks.summary: Write the missing practical usage guide for the Project Control Gateway.
tasks.description: Create ai-system/project-control/08-usage-guide.md and reconcile it through plan, task, prompt and documentation controls.
tasks.active_role: AI System Maintainer + Technical Writer AI
tasks.expected_result: Usage guide implemented, validation passed, waiting for Human Owner acceptance.
scope: Create ai-system/project-control/08-usage-guide.md.
Explain how Human Owner, ChatGPT Orchestrator, Codex Executor, and Python CLIs interact.
Explain daily usage of planctl.py, taskctl.py, docctl.py, and evolutionctl.py.
Explain when to create Initiative, Epic, and Task records.
Explain when to use docctl.py and how CODEX_PROMPT.md fits into execution.
Explain protected files and what Codex must never edit manually.
Include practical command examples, common flows, troubleshooting, and final validation checklist.
out_of_scope: Do not implement SOP orchestration.
Do not implement subagents.
Do not refactor evolutionctl.py.
Do not add new docctl.py features.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/state/**.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/events/**.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/generated/**.
acceptance_criteria: 08-usage-guide.md exists and uses the same practical style as neighboring project-control documents.
The guide explains role interactions, CLI responsibilities, daily workflow, documentation workflow, protected-file boundaries, troubleshooting, and validation.
Only supported CLI commands are shown as exact command examples.
The document is registered through docctl.py and moved no further than review without Human Owner acceptance.
Plan, task, documentation, smoke, and protected-files validation commands pass.
review_instructions: Verify that no protected project-control files were edited manually.
Verify that the task and document are not marked Human-approved or active/accepted.
Verify that unsupported commands are not presented as executable examples.
notes: Human Owner requested one-pass execution with final state waiting for owner acceptance.
tasks.approval_notes: Accepted by Human Owner after review.

tasks.title: Document Skills Layer Roadmap
tasks.summary: Create a controlled skills layer documentation page that records useful project skills/plugins and recommends next skills to create.
tasks.description: Create ai-system/skills/README.md as a controlled documentation page for skills/plugins in this repository. Register the document with docctl.py in a non-active lifecycle state and validate project-control outputs.
tasks.active_role: AI System Maintainer + Technical Writer AI
tasks.expected_result: Skills layer roadmap document created, registered in documentation control, generated prompt package written, validation passed, waiting for Human Owner approval.
scope: Create ai-system/skills/README.md.
Explain what skills/plugins are for in this project.
State that skills/plugins are guidance and routing helpers, not sources of authority.
State that Python CLI remains the source of truth.
List existing useful skills: Project Control Gateway Skill and CLI Creator Skill.

List recommended skills to create: Documentation Control Skill, SOP Authoring Skill, Agent Assignment Skill, Review Gate Skill, QA Evidence Skill, Decision / ADR Skill, Git Safety Skill, Protected Files Skill.
For each skill, include purpose, related CLI, priority, allowed actions, and forbidden actions.
Include a recommended creation order.
Include rules requiring every new skill to be created through a controlled Task and requiring subagents to obey CLI/source-of-truth rules.
out_of_scope: Do not implement new skills.
Do not mark the document active or accepted without Human Owner approval.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/state/**.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/events/**.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/generated/**.
Do not change project-control CLI behavior.
acceptance_criteria: ai-system/skills/README.md exists and covers skills/plugins as guidance and routing helpers, not authoritative source documents.
The document clearly states that Python CLIs and registered source documents remain the source of truth.
The document lists the two existing useful skills and the eight recommended future skills with purpose, related CLI, priority, allowed actions, and forbidden actions.
The document includes a recommended skill creation order.
The document states that every new skill must be created through a controlled Task.
The document states that subagents may use skills only as guidance and must still obey CLI/source-of-truth rules.
The document is registered through docctl.py as draft or review, not active.
Plan, task, documentation, smoke doc-control, and protected-files validation commands pass.
review_instructions: Verify that no protected project-control files were edited manually.
Verify that no new skills were implemented in this task.
Verify that the document remains in draft or review status until Human Owner approval.
notes: Human Owner explicitly requested a project-controlled documentation task and allowed completion to review state without self-approval.
tasks.approval_notes: Accepted by Human Owner after review.

tasks.title: Create Clarification Gate Skill
tasks.summary: Create a high-priority Clarification Gate Skill for Codex and subagents, defining when to ask the Human Owner versus inspect or proceed with safe assumptions.
tasks.description: Create .agents/skills/clarification-gate/SKILL.md and update the skills README if present. Route project-control state changes through CLI commands only. Do not self-approve the task, document, or skill as accepted or active.
tasks.active_role: AI System Maintainer + Prompt/Skill Engineer
tasks.expected_result: Clarification Gate Skill created; skills README updated if available; validation completed; result remains awaiting Human Owner acceptance.
scope: Create .agents/skills/clarification-gate/SKILL.md with blocker severity, question budget, and subagent guidance.
Update ai-system/skills/README.md if it exists to recommend the Clarification Gate Skill as high priority.
Use docctl.py registration only if the documentation registry is intended to track .agents/skills files.
Run required planctl.py, taskctl.py, docctl.py, smoke, and protected-files validations.
out_of_scope: Manual edits to AI_PROJECT/state/**, AI_PROJECT/events/**, or AI_PROJECT/generated/**.
Implementation changes to planctl.py, taskctl.py, docctl.py, or evolutionctl.py unless blocked.
Self-approving the task, document, or skill as accepted, approved, done, or active.
acceptance_criteria: Skill explains that it prevents premature execution on ambiguous or unsafe requests.
Skill states the core rule: inspect first, ask only when blocked.
Skill defines when to ask before task creation, during task execution, and when not to ask.
Skill includes critical blocker, non-critical ambiguity, and inspectable ambiguity severity model with examples.
Skill includes a question budget and forbids using clarification questions to avoid normal inspection.
Skills README recommends the Clarification Gate Skill as high priority if the README exists.
Required validation commands complete or any blocker is reported.
review_instructions: Review for compliance with Human Owner approval boundaries and protected project-control rules.
Do not mark accepted, approved, active, or done without Human Owner decision.
tasks.approval_notes: Accepted by Human Owner after review.

tasks.active_role: Codex Executor

Use existing task/evolution state schemas, generated prompt conventions and audit-style JSONL events.

Do not modify unrelated AI system documents or product source code.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/codexctl.py exists and uses only Python standard library.
status works without crashing and reports READY or BLOCKED state with stable codes.
build --task can generate CODEX_PROMPT.md for an executable task and fails clearly for invalid task IDs or statuses.
build --change can generate CODEX_PROMPT.md for approved CHG-001 and fails clearly for invalid change IDs or statuses.
clear invalidates or removes the current Codex prompt package without modifying source-of-truth task or evolution state.
Required validation commands from the owner prompt pass or any blocker is reported clearly.
review_instructions: Review for lifecycle boundary enforcement, prompt completeness, generated artifact consistency and standard-library-only implementation.
tasks.approval_notes: Reviewed and accepted

Add or specify document status/frontmatter synchronization checks.
Add content hash tracking for registered documents.
Add last reviewed content hash tracking.
Improve DOCS_GAPS.md categories so gaps are actionable.

Keep AI_PROJECT/state/docs.json authoritative and mutable only through docctl.py.

Do not implement vector search.
Do not change codexctl.py behavior.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/state/**, AI_PROJECT/events/** or AI_PROJECT/generated/**.

docctl tracks current document content hash.

docctl can register or account for root-level documents and skills without treating generated files as authoritative source docs.

tasks.title: P1 Implement contextctl deterministic retrieval MVP
tasks.summary: Create a new controlled CLI gateway that builds deterministic Context Packs from registered documents without vector search.
tasks.description: Implement scripts/contextctl.py as a dependency-light deterministic retrieval layer. It should use docs.json and registered source documents to build a reproducible keyword/metadata index and generate CONTEXT_PACK.md for a task or query.
tasks.active_role: Codex Executor
tasks.expected_result: A deterministic contextctl MVP can build and validate generated Context Packs without vector search.
scope: Create scripts/contextctl.py.
Add deterministic Markdown chunking by document headings or another stable rule.
Build a local derived index from registered docs.
Implement keyword/metadata search.
Generate AI_PROJECT/generated/CONTEXT_PACK.md.
Generate AI_PROJECT/generated/CONTEXT_STATUS.md if useful.
Write AI_PROJECT/events/context-events.jsonl for context build events if generated output is written.
Exclude generated, archived, deprecated, template and example documents by default unless explicitly allowed.
Include selected source paths, headings, content hashes, chunk hashes and selection reasons in the Context Pack.
Add validation/check-generated behavior for context outputs.
Add smoke test coverage if the repository has an appropriate smoke test pattern.
out_of_scope: Do not add vector DB or embeddings.
Do not call external APIs.
Do not modify codexctl.py integration yet.
Do not make context pack a source of truth.
Do not expand task allowed_files through retrieved context.
acceptance_criteria: contextctl.py uses only Python standard library.
contextctl can build or refresh a deterministic derived index.
contextctl can search registered source docs by query.
contextctl can build a Context Pack for a task ID or explicit query.
Context Pack includes selected documents/sections, reasons, hashes and source metadata.
Context Pack excludes generated files and inactive/archived docs by default.
Context Pack clearly states that it is generated output and not source of truth.
check-generated or equivalent detects stale generated context output.
Validation/smoke commands pass or blockers are reported.
review_instructions: Report how deterministic scoring works, what files are indexed by default, what is excluded, and how stale context is detected.
tasks.approval_notes: Reviewed and accepted by Human Owner

Add clear errors for missing, stale or invalid context pack.
Update prompt package documentation if needed.
Add smoke/validation coverage.
out_of_scope: Do not implement vector search.
Do not change task lifecycle rules.
Do not make codexctl.py responsible for document indexing.
Do not allow retrieved context to override task fields.
Do not manually edit generated CODEX_PROMPT.md.

codexctl fails clearly if the context pack is missing, stale or invalid.
Generated CODEX_PROMPT.md records context pack path/hash and source metadata.
Prompt explicitly states that retrieved context is read-only.
Prompt explicitly states that context does not expand allowed files, scope or acceptance criteria.
codexctl remains able to build prompts without context pack when requested.
Required validation/smoke commands pass or blockers are reported.

tasks.title: P3 Add optional vector backend for contextctl
tasks.summary: Add optional vector or hybrid retrieval backend after deterministic retrieval exists.

Keep keyword backend as default.
Add optional vector backend only behind explicit dependency/feature guard.
Add metadata/hash validation for vector chunks.
Detect stale embeddings when source chunk hash changes.
Add hybrid scoring rules if practical.
Add privacy guardrails for external embeddings.
Document vector backend as derived cache, not source of truth.
out_of_scope: Do not require vector dependencies for normal validation.
Do not call external embedding APIs by default.
Do not store secrets.
Do not make vector index authoritative.
Do not remove deterministic keyword backend.
allowed_files: .gitignore if needed for local vector cache paths
requirements or project dependency file only if one already exists and the task explicitly justifies it
acceptance_criteria: Keyword backend remains default and works without optional dependencies.
Vector backend is opt-in.
External embeddings are disabled by default.
Vector index stores source path, heading, chunk hash, content hash and embedding metadata.
Stale vector chunks are detected when source content changes.
Vector backend is documented as derived cache, not source of truth.
Validation passes in environments without vector dependencies.

review_instructions: Report optional dependency strategy, privacy/security guardrails, cache paths, stale embedding detection and fallback behavior.

notes: Logical label: TIG-01
Add explicit Human Owner decisions for dependency satisfaction, reparenting, epic.key immutability and legacy TASK-XXX visibility.

Human Owner design decisions: hard dependencies are satisfied only by done; task reparenting is forbidden in MVP; epic.key is immutable after task refs exist; locks/resources are postponed unless required by TIG-05; legacy TASK-XXX remains resolvable during compatibility period.

tasks.active_role: Codex Executor + Documentation Control Maintainer

scope: Inspect docctl.py behavior and documentation registry state to identify why DOCS_GAPS.md is stale.
Refresh documentation generated files through docctl.py only.
Run documentation generated checks and protected-files validation.
out_of_scope: Do not change task identity, dependency graph, execution queue, epic keys, or task resolver behavior.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/state/**, AI_PROJECT/events/**, or AI_PROJECT/generated/**.
allowed_files: scripts/docctl.py only if the drift is caused by a docctl rendering bug
acceptance_criteria: python scripts/docctl.py validate passes.
python scripts/docctl.py render completes successfully.
python scripts/docctl.py check-generated passes.
python scripts/check-protected-project-files.py --verbose no longer reports DOCS_GAPS.md drift.
No protected AI_PROJECT files are manually edited.
review_instructions: Verify that DOCS_GAPS.md was updated only through docctl.py unless a docctl rendering bug required a scoped scripts/docctl.py fix.
Verify that TIG/task identity behavior was not modified.
tasks.approval_notes: Documentation generated drift cleanup reviewed and accepted. DOCS_GAPS.md was refreshed through docctl.py render; protected-files validation is clean.

tasks.title: Task A - Inventory existing ctl commands and state mutation paths
tasks.summary: Map current ctl scripts, commands, state files, event logs, generated outputs, and direct mutation risks.
tasks.description: Inventory all current project-control command surfaces before any unification work. The result should make existing write paths, duplicated lifecycle logic, and unsafe direct mutation paths visible.
tasks.active_role: AI System Maintainer / Project Control Architect
tasks.expected_result: Inventory report produced; no code behavior changed.
scope: Identify all scripts/*ctl.py files and their command domains.
List supported commands by domain and classify read, write, render, validation, and audit behavior.
Identify duplicated lifecycle logic and direct state/events/generated mutation paths.
Identify task ID collision risks across epics and aliases.
Produce a readable inventory artifact if an approved standard location exists.
out_of_scope: Do not implement aictl.py.
Do not refactor existing ctl scripts.
Do not create web app files.
Do not manually edit AI_PROJECT/state/**, AI_PROJECT/events/**, or AI_PROJECT/generated/**.
acceptance_criteria: Existing ctl surface is documented.
Write paths are known.
Gaps and unsafe paths are listed.
No code behavior changed.
review_instructions: Verify that protected project-control files were changed only through the owning CLI.
Verify that this is inventory only and does not alter ctl script behavior.
notes: Phase 1 - Planning and design.
Future implementation tasks that change AI Development System behavior require the approved evolution path before execution.
tasks.approval_notes: Inventory report accepted. Findings are sufficient for control-plane architecture design.

tasks.title: Task B - Design unified control-plane architecture
tasks.summary: Record the target architecture for a shared command/service layer before implementation.
tasks.description: Design the future scripts/aictl.py entrypoint and ai_project_ctl package structure, including store, events, validation, registry, renderer, IDs, locks, domain services, and web shell boundaries.
tasks.active_role: AI System Maintainer / Project Control Architect
tasks.expected_result: Architecture recorded in an approved design artifact; no implementation yet.
scope: Describe scripts/aictl.py as the preferred unified CLI facade.
Describe ai_project_ctl/core services for store, events, validation, registry, renderer, ids, and locks.
Describe ai_project_ctl/domains services for tasks, epics, changes, reviews, evolution, codex, and context.
Describe ai_project_ctl/web as a thin local UI over the same command registry.
State the transaction rule: Command -> Validate -> Append Event -> Mutate State -> Regenerate Views -> Return Result.
State that generated/*.md is derived and must not be edited manually.
out_of_scope: Do not create ai_project_ctl package files.
Do not create scripts/aictl.py.
Do not change existing ctl behavior.
Do not bypass evolution approval for future source changes.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture is recorded in the plan/task description or an approved design artifact.
Design explicitly states that Web UI cannot bypass the command layer.
Design explicitly states generated/*.md is derived and must not be edited manually.
No implementation files are created in this design task.
review_instructions: Verify that the architecture preserves existing project-control source-of-truth boundaries.
Verify that future implementation still requires controlled evolution approval where behavior changes.
notes: Phase 1 - Planning and design.
Depends conceptually on TASK-019 inventory findings.

depends_on.reason: Architecture design depends on the current ctl inventory.

tasks.approval_notes: Unified control-plane architecture accepted. Design is sufficient for ID allocation strategy task.

tasks.title: Task C - Add global ID allocation strategy
tasks.summary: Define a global ID allocation model that prevents task conflicts across parallel epics and future web actions.
tasks.description: Specify whether IDs are globally unique or scoped, prefer globally unique TASK-001 style task IDs, define atomic allocation behavior, and define validation for duplicate task IDs across all epics.
tasks.active_role: AI System Maintainer / Project Control Architect
tasks.expected_result: ID allocation policy recorded with a clear future implementation path.
scope: Define global versus scoped ID policy for tasks, epics, changes, and command registry entities.
Prefer globally unique task IDs while preserving existing aliases and epic-scoped refs.
Design atomic ID allocation for CLI and future web write actions.
Design validation that prevents duplicate task IDs across all epics.
Define whether ID allocation should write audit events.
out_of_scope: Do not implement ids.py.
Do not migrate existing task IDs.
Do not change taskctl.py behavior in this design task.
acceptance_criteria: The plan explicitly resolves cross-epic task ID collision risk.
There is a clear future implementation path for ids.py or equivalent.
Parallel execution risk is accounted for.
No ID migration or code behavior change is performed in this design task.
review_instructions: Verify that the design is compatible with existing legacy IDs, task aliases, and epic keys.
Verify that protected project-control files were not manually edited.
notes: Phase 1 - Planning and design.
Future implementation must preserve existing TASK-* references and aliases.

depends_on.reason: ID allocation strategy depends on the current ctl inventory.

tasks.approval_notes: Global ID allocation strategy accepted. Identity, ref, UID, alias, locking, validation, and parallel allocation rules are sufficient for core package design.

tasks.title: Task D - Introduce ai_project_ctl core package
tasks.summary: Create shared internal services that future CLI and Web UI both use.
tasks.description: Implement the core control-plane package for store loading/saving, audit events, validation, rendering triggers, result objects, error model, locking, and atomic writes while preserving existing behavior.
tasks.active_role: Backend Developer AI / AI System Maintainer
tasks.expected_result: Shared core services exist with tests and existing ctl behavior remains compatible.
scope: Create ai_project_ctl/core store, events, validation, registry, renderer, ids, locks, result, and error support as needed.
Move shared state loading, saving, event append, validation hooks, rendering triggers, and atomic write helpers into reusable services.
Add tests for core package behavior.
Preserve current planctl.py, taskctl.py, docctl.py, contextctl.py, codexctl.py, and evolutionctl.py behavior.
out_of_scope: Do not replace all old ctl scripts in this task.
Do not add web UI files.
Do not change lifecycle rules beyond approved scope.
acceptance_criteria: Existing behavior remains compatible.
Core package has tests.
No domain command should need to write state directly after migration path is established.
State mutation still goes through validated CLI/service paths.
review_instructions: Verify behavior compatibility for existing ctl commands.
Verify tests cover atomic writes, event append, validation errors, and render trigger behavior where implemented.
notes: Phase 2 - CLI foundation.
Requires approved evolution change before execution because this modifies scripts and control behavior.

depends_on.reason: Core package implementation depends on the approved architecture design.
Core package implementation depends on the global ID allocation strategy.

tasks.approval_notes: ai_project_ctl core package foundation accepted. Existing ctl behavior preserved; tests and validation passed.

tasks.title: Task E - Add command registry
tasks.summary: Make project-control operations discoverable and callable through one registry.
tasks.description: Introduce command metadata with names, descriptions, argument schema, read/write metadata, and output format metadata so both CLI and future web UI can discover and describe supported operations.
tasks.active_role: Backend Developer AI / AI System Maintainer
tasks.expected_result: Command registry supports listing and describing project-control commands.
scope: Implement command registry metadata for task, epic, change, review, context, codex, project, and command domains where available.
Support example names such as task.list, task.show, task.create, task.transition, epic.list, change.create, context.build, codex.prompt.build, project.doctor, command.list, and command.describe.
Expose command names, descriptions, args schema, read/write metadata, and output format metadata.
Add tests for registry listing and command description behavior.
out_of_scope: Do not implement web forms in this task.
Do not remove existing ctl entrypoints.
Do not add unsupported lifecycle commands.
acceptance_criteria: Commands have names, descriptions, args schema, read/write metadata, and output format metadata.
CLI can list and describe commands.
Web UI can later use the registry to render actions/forms.
Registry does not bypass domain validation.
review_instructions: Verify that registry metadata cannot execute unsupported or unvalidated operations.
Verify command descriptions match implemented behavior.
notes: Phase 2 - CLI foundation.
Requires approved evolution change before execution because command catalog behavior changes.

depends_on.reason: Command registry depends on shared core services.

tasks.approval_notes: Command registry foundation accepted. Registry is metadata-only, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.

tasks.title: Task F - Implement unified CLI facade scripts/aictl.py
tasks.summary: Create one preferred entrypoint for project-control operations.
tasks.description: Add scripts/aictl.py as a unified CLI facade over shared domain services, supporting human-readable output and --json automation output while preserving existing taskctl/evolutionctl/docctl/contextctl/codexctl behavior.
tasks.active_role: Backend Developer AI / AI System Maintainer
tasks.expected_result: aictl facade works through shared services and existing ctl scripts remain functional.
scope: Implement commands such as task list, task show, task transition, epic list, context build, codex prompt build, project doctor, project render, command list, and command describe as supported by shared services.
Support human-readable output.
Support --json output for automation.
Ensure aictl calls shared domain services and command registry instead of duplicating lifecycle logic.
Add tests or smoke coverage for representative read and write commands.
out_of_scope: Do not remove legacy ctl scripts.
Do not add web UI in this task.
Do not implement unsupported commands without validation.
acceptance_criteria: aictl can call shared domain services.
aictl supports human-readable output.
aictl supports --json for automation.
Existing taskctl/evolutionctl behavior is not broken.
review_instructions: Verify aictl delegates to validated shared services and does not mutate protected files directly.
Verify existing ctl command compatibility after aictl is introduced.
notes: Phase 2 - CLI foundation.
Requires approved evolution change before execution because this adds the preferred project-control entrypoint.

depends_on.reason: Unified CLI facade depends on command registry metadata.

tasks.approval_notes: Unified aictl facade accepted. Facade delegates to existing ctl scripts, registry discovery works, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.

tasks.title: Task G - Convert old ctl scripts into compatibility wrappers
tasks.summary: Keep existing ctl workflows working while centralizing logic.
tasks.description: Update taskctl.py, evolutionctl.py, planctl.py, docctl.py, contextctl.py, and codexctl.py where practical so they delegate to ai_project_ctl domain services or aictl-compatible services without breaking existing command syntax.
tasks.active_role: Backend Developer AI / AI System Maintainer
tasks.expected_result: Legacy ctl scripts remain supported while shared services own behavior.
scope: Make taskctl.py delegate to ai_project_ctl domains or shared services where practical.
Make evolutionctl.py and other ctl scripts delegate where practical.
Keep old command syntax compatible unless a separate approved migration says otherwise.
Mark wrappers as legacy-compatible but supported in documentation or code comments where appropriate.
Reduce duplicate lifecycle validation where avoidable.
out_of_scope: Do not remove existing ctl scripts.
Do not change public command syntax unless explicitly approved.
Do not implement web UI in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Existing commands still work.
New code path is centralized.
No duplicate lifecycle validation remains where avoidable.
Wrapper compatibility is documented or obvious from implementation.
review_instructions: Run representative compatibility checks for old ctl commands.
Verify protected state mutation still goes only through approved service paths.
notes: Phase 2 - CLI foundation.
Requires approved evolution change before execution because existing ctl behavior is refactored.

depends_on.reason: Compatibility wrappers depend on the unified CLI/service facade.

tasks.approval_notes: Legacy ctl compatibility wrapper migration accepted. Shared plumbing extracted safely; domain behavior, command syntax, lifecycle gates, protected-file rules, and anti-recursion boundaries preserved.

tasks.title: Task H - Add project doctor command
tasks.summary: Provide one health check for project-control state.
tasks.description: Add a project doctor command that reports PASS/WARN/FAIL for duplicate IDs, invalid statuses, lifecycle residue, missing parents, stale generated files, stale Codex outputs, event/state assumptions, missing generated views, and detectable direct mutation risk.
tasks.active_role: Backend Developer AI / QA Engineer AI
tasks.expected_result: Project doctor reports useful PASS/WARN/FAIL health output and exits non-zero on FAIL.
scope: Implement python scripts/aictl.py project doctor or equivalent command registry route.

Check duplicate IDs, invalid task status, invalid lifecycle transition residue, missing parent epic, stale generated files, stale CODEX_PROMPT.md or CODEX_STATUS.md, event/state assumptions, missing generated views, and unsupported direct mutations where detectable.
Return useful human-readable and automation-friendly output.
Add tests or smoke checks for PASS, WARN, and FAIL scenarios.
out_of_scope: Do not repair state automatically unless a supported render command is explicitly invoked.
Do not edit protected files manually.
Do not add web UI in this task.
acceptance_criteria: python scripts/aictl.py project doctor reports PASS/WARN/FAIL.
Doctor exits non-zero on FAIL.
Output is useful for both humans and CI.
Doctor does not bypass owning CLI validation or render commands.
review_instructions: Verify doctor failures are deterministic and actionable.
Verify doctor does not mutate semantic state unexpectedly.
notes: Phase 3 - Safety and validation.
Requires approved evolution change before execution because this adds validation behavior.

depends_on.reason: Project doctor depends on the unified CLI facade.

tasks.approval_notes: Project doctor diagnostics accepted. PASS/WARN/FAIL model implemented; hard validation delegated to owning CLIs; stale prompt/context artifacts correctly reported as warnings.

tasks.title: Task I - Add locking and atomic write protection
tasks.summary: Make parallel project-control operations safer.
tasks.description: Add file locking and atomic write behavior for state, events, and generated outputs so concurrent operations cannot corrupt project-control files or leave partial writes.
tasks.active_role: Backend Developer AI / DevOps Engineer AI
tasks.expected_result: Concurrent writes are guarded by locks and atomic replace semantics with readable errors.
scope: Add file lock support for state mutations.
Use atomic write via temp file plus replace for protected outputs where appropriate.
Return clear errors if another process is writing.
Avoid partial writes to state/events/generated files.
Add tests for lock contention and atomic write behavior.
out_of_scope: Do not introduce a database backend.
Do not implement remote or multi-user server locking.
Do not change lifecycle semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent writes cannot corrupt state.
Locking behavior is tested.
Error messages are readable.
State/events/generated writes avoid partial output.
review_instructions: Verify lock implementation is cross-platform enough for the repository's expected local workflow.
Verify tests cover contention and failure cleanup.
notes: Phase 3 - Safety and validation.
Requires approved evolution change before execution because project-control write semantics change.

depends_on.reason: Locking and atomic writes depend on shared core services.

tasks.approval_notes: Locking and atomic write protection accepted. FileLock, atomic writes, audit append fsync, tests, and compatibility checks passed.

tasks.title: Task J - Add read-only local Web Control Center MVP
tasks.summary: Provide initial web visibility without mutation risk.
tasks.description: Add a read-only local Control Center, preferably FastAPI + Jinja2 + HTMX unless repository constraints suggest otherwise, launched through python scripts/aictl.py web --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765 and backed by the same command registry.
tasks.active_role: Frontend Developer AI / Backend Developer AI

Clearly show current task, queues, stale generated files, and health status.

out_of_scope: Do not add web write actions in this task.
Do not add authentication or remote hosted deployment.
Do not edit JSON directly from route handlers.

Web UI uses the same command/core layer as CLI.

tasks.approval_notes: Read-only local Web Control Center MVP accepted. UI is loopback-only, GET/HEAD-only, generated files are labeled derived, and no web mutation paths were added.

tasks.title: Task K - Add controlled Web write actions
tasks.summary: Allow safe project-control state changes from UI only after the read-only MVP is stable.
